_comment: Build configuration tracker for gozik-yt-music Nuitka packaging. Auto-updated by package.sh post-build.

project.name: gozik-yt-music-server
project.description: YouTube Music gRPC plugin server for the gozik desktop player

binary.name: gozik-yt-music-server
binary.extraction_note: Payload extracted to $XDG_CACHE_HOME on first launch. Subsequent launches reuse the cached tree (keyed by content hash).

--mode=onefile.rationale: Nuitka 4.x unified mode. Implies standalone and compresses output into a single zstd ELF. Replaces legacy --onefile + --standalone dual-flag from Nuitka 1.x/2.x.

--onefile-tempdir-spec={CACHE_DIR}/gozik.rationale: Overrides default /tmp extraction to XDG_CACHE_HOME. {VERSION}=content hash prevents cross-version collisions. Avoids noexec /tmp failures.

--remove-output.rationale: Purges intermediate .build/ after ELF assembly. Release mode only; omitted in --debug mode.

--lto=yes.rationale: Link-Time Optimisation. Reduces binary size ~5%, enables cross-TU inlining. Adds ~30% compile time. Release mode only.

--include-package=ytmusicapi.rationale: Dynamic mixin loading and importlib.resources; static tracer misses auth.oauth, mixins.*, models.*.

--include-package=grpc.rationale: 56 submodules discovered via __import__ strings at runtime. Explicit inclusion required.

--include-package=grpc._cython.rationale: Cython extension (_cygrpc.so) is referenced only at runtime; belt-and-suspenders for the namespace.

--include-package=google.protobuf.rationale: protobuf 4+ C-extension fast path + pure-Python fallback; google namespace needs __init__ shims.

--include-package=google.protobuf.intern.rationale: Internal namespace skipped by static analysis; serialiser reflection references it heavily.

--include-package=yt_dlp.rationale: 1100+ submodules via plugin discovery strings. Partial inclusion causes runtime ImportError.

--include-package=yt_dlp.extractor.rationale: Belt-and-suspenders for the largest dynamically-loaded yt-dlp subpackage.

--include-package=requests,certifi,chars.rationale: ytmusicapi HTTP stack. Explicit inclusion guards against namespace-package detection failures.

--include-package=generated,handlers.rationale: Project-local packages not discoverable as installed packages.

--include-package-data=ytmusicapi,grpc.rationale: Package-internal data files (schema JSON, locale data) from wheels.

--include-data-files=certifi/cacert.pem.rationale: certifi.where() must resolve inside the extraction root. Required for all HTTPS connections.

--include-data-dir=generated.rationale: Protobuf stub files; __init__.py namespace anchor must be present post-extraction.

--nofollow-import-to=tkinter,unittest,te.rationale: All confirmed unused at server runtime. Exclusion saves ~40 MB payload.

--python-flag=no_site.rationale: Prevents system site-packages from shadowing bundled deps on startup.

--python-flag=no_warnings.rationale: Suppresses DeprecationWarning from protobuf/grpc internals in production binary.

--enable-plugins=anti-bloat.rationale: Strips Jupyter kernels, test harnesses. Reduces binary 15-30%.

--assume-yes-for-downloads.rationale: Suppresses CPython base-archive download prompt for CI runners.

--jobs=nproc.rationale: Parallelises C compilation across all available cores.

excluded_packages.tkinter: GUI toolkit — headless gRPC server never renders a GUI
excluded_packages.unittest: Test framework — not needed at runtime
excluded_packages.grpc_tools: Build-time proto compiler — must not ship in runtime binary

data_files.description: Mozilla CA certificate bundle (PEM); required for HTTPS by ytmusicapi and yt-dlp
Compiled protobuf Python stubs for music_provider and provider_link services

build_requirements.patchelf: 0.14+ (tested: 0.18.0); required for RPATH rewriting in onefile ELF on Linux
build_requirements.ccache: optional (tested: 4.11.2); auto-detected by Nuitka when on PATH
build_requirements.upx: NOT used — incompatible with Nuitka onefile zstd payloads and triggers AV false positives
build_requirements.disk_build: ~2 GB (yt-dlp 1128-module C compilation is I/O intensive)

post_build.note: Populated by package.sh after a successful build.
